architecture.summary: A text-to-text pre-trained language model augmented with a large memory of question-answer pairs. The system retrieves relevant QA pairs from memory, and a pre-trained retrieval component learns to select which pairs are relevant to an input question. Retrieved pairs are concatenated as context and passed to the text-to-text model to generate the final answer. The system supports multi-hop reasoning by iteratively retrieving and reasoning over multiple QA pairs.

biblio.title: Augmenting Pre-trained Language Models with QA-Memory for Open-Domain Question Answering

claims.statement: A new pre-training task for the latent step of question retrieval substantially simplifies training and greatly improves performance on smaller QA benchmarks

Question-answer pairs provide practical advantages including being more memory and compute-efficient compared to existing open-book ODQA methods that retrieve from large text corpora or knowledge bases
The QA system can answer multi-hop questions that do not explicitly appear in the collection of stored question-answer pairs
